groups.title: Pre-built escalating sexting scripts are the sales unit
Send 1–2 free teaser images before any PPV offer to prime the subscriber
Build a fan profile through scripted questions before any PPV
Log personal fan details in notes so any chatter can continue the relationship seamlessly
Inject scripted rapport breaks and push-pull frames throughout chat to create chase dynamic
Rapport, transition, then sell
Never send mass PPVs — individual messaging generates 3-4x more revenue
Segment subscriber lists by content preference and renewal status to send targeted PPV messages
Train chatters with agency-owned PPV scripts rather than relying on their defaults
Live-call training with seconds-fast feedback loops and single-point focus
Weekly async Loom chat reviews focused on new subscribers + high-ticket purchases
Agency founder must chatter personally before hiring chatters
Configure an auto-sent paid intro message ($15–25) to fire on every new subscriber
Three-tier commonality system to manufacture emotional bond
Never send multiple follow-up messages to a non-responding subscriber
Anchor the PPV ladder at $15 entry point to minimize first-purchase friction
Hard floor of 50/50 revenue split — never go lower
Free-trial subs, monetize later via PPV — not on the sub price
Price dynamically off purchase history
Never fold on PPV price — repackage content instead of discounting
Match deal structure to creator tier, not one-size 50/50
Set hard minimum PPV prices: $19.99 images, $39.99 videos, $300 customs
7-day IG warm-up: algorithm training, niche engagement, delayed link
Differentiate every signal across repurposing accounts to prevent IG cluster-ban
Make repurposed Reels structurally different, not just color-adjusted
Two-funnel X system: paid retweet drops plus comment-baiting
Main brand account fed by disposable burner accounts
Snapchat + read-only Telegram channel as a ban-resilient backup funnel
Build a perpetual stockpile of karma-farmed Reddit accounts via VA team
Contract out Reddit and X content distribution as a low-overhead traffic stream
Build a Telegram-sourced S4S network before signing creators to pre-load launch traffic
Cold outreach is a volume-consistency-conversation game
Use an Intrigue-Curiosity-CTA opener and book the call fast
DM prospects from a personal-looking account, not an agency page
Source creators across IG, Twitter, Reddit, TikTok, and prioritize referrals
Target mid-tier creators (10K followers, ~$1-3K/mo) not top earners
Qualify creators against a checklist and disqualify on red flags
Personal/agency brand is the real acquisition constraint
Predictable creator-signing via paid ad funnel
Revenue-gated hiring ladder: VAs, then chat manager, then ops
Replace yourself via ownership ladder, not task delegation
Loom-video SOPs to eliminate training misinterpretation
Daily feedback cadence to accelerate employee ramp 7x vs weekly
Run three departments (acquisition, marketing, chatting) each under a manager
Delegate menial work to protect creative output
Run a structured onboarding call covering 6 key areas before going live
Build a model profile doc with chatting style, persona facts, and content inventory for chatters
Run 3-variant script tests with unlock-rate tracking before standardising any chatting SOP
Weekly cadence and standards prevent regression to chaos
Manage creator expectations week-by-week or they invent their own
Central account/team/finance trackers to remove owner from ops
Treat the vault as inventory: weekly script refresh and analytics pruning
Generate a formal invoice every time a model payment is made to maintain a legal paper trail
A defined niche, not looks or tech, separates earners
A cheap competitive model wins on marketing, not on max realism
Use a five-category taxonomy to separate OF feed, PPV, SFW social, dating-app, and Reddit-specific content
Batch shoot 5 looks → batch schedule 5 days of page posts + paired PPV messages
Give every model a written character
Build distinct creator brands (audience + identity + story) — not just niches
Keep the profile wall teasing, never explicit, to preserve PPV value
Treat every revenue-generating asset as likely to disappear tomorrow; diversify across creators, accounts, and traffic sources
AI-image watermarks (SynthID) are the new ban trigger; switch generators
Bans driven by unbranded sexual content, not IP/proxies
An AI chatbot can replace much of the chat team at a fraction of the cost
Real creator + AI clone, kept on OnlyFans
Real models beat AI on connection, customs and platform access
Hold weekly video calls with each creator to give account updates and collect feedback
Do every role yourself first, document it, then hand it off
High revenue means nothing without margin — $100k revenue can yield $3k profit
